commit.message:
feat(stdlib): add std::deque module (#210)

Expose a double-ended queue (deque) as an opaque handle type in
the stdlib.  The runtime stores i64 values in a VecDeque behind
a heap-allocated pointer.

New files:
- hew-runtime/src/vecdeque.rs  — #[no_mangle] FFI functions
- std/deque.hew                — opaque Deque type with methods
- E2E test exercising push_front, push_back, pop_front, pop_back,
  len, is_empty, and free

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: hew
repository.description: A statically-typed, actor-oriented programming language for concurrent and distributed systems.

commit.message:
feat(stdlib): add std/iter with functional-style Vec<int> operations (#206)

Add std/iter module providing map_int, filter_int, fold_int, any, all,
and sum as pure Hew implementations for Vec<int>. These fill the gap
until a full Iterator trait is available.

Includes E2E test covering all functions plus empty-vector edge cases.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Replace emoji with inline SVG icons, add session persistence, add favicon (#20)

* Initial plan

* Replace emoji icons with inline SVGs, add session persistence, add favicon

1. Replace all emoji icons (☀️/🌙, 👆, ⚙, 📁, ⚠, ↗, 🖥️, ⬇, ◀/▶) with
   clean inline SVG icons in the dashboard and toolbar.
2. Add session persistence via SessionManager.setSavePath() / restore() so
   sessions survive backend process restarts. Sessions are saved to
   ~/.lengcat-vst/sessions.json on every mutation.
3. Add an SVG favicon (embedded as data URI) to both dashboard and login pages.
4. Add unit tests for all new functionality.

Co-authored-by: Shaloc <24523674+Shaloc@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add comment to restore() catch block per code review

Co-authored-by: Shaloc <24523674+Shaloc@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(stdlib): add std::encoding::xml for XML parsing and serialization (#209)

* feat(stdlib): add std::encoding::xml for XML parsing and serialization

Implement the std::encoding::xml module wrapping the quick-xml crate
via Rust FFI, following the same pattern as std::encoding::json.

API:
- xml.parse(s) — parse XML string into a Node tree
- node.to_string() — serialize back to XML
- node.get_attribute(name) — get attribute value (empty if missing)
- node.get_child(index) — get child node by index
- node.get_text() — concatenated text content (recursive)
- node.get_tag() — element tag name
- node.children_count() — number of child nodes
- node.is_element() — element vs text node check
- node.free() — release node resources

Includes:
- Rust staticlib with 9 unit tests
- E2E test parsing a document with attributes, children, and text
- Registered in workspace Cargo.toml, Makefile, and CMakeLists.txt

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: add hew-std-encoding-xml to CI stdlib build and apply formatting

Merge main, add hew-std-encoding-xml to CI workflow's stdlib build step,
and apply cargo fmt to fix function signature formatting.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(stdlib): add std/math module with integer and float operations (#212)

Add a new std::math stdlib module providing:

Integer functions: abs, min, max, clamp, sign
Float functions: abs_f, sqrt, pow, floor, ceil, round
Constants: pi(), e()

The codegen backend maps math.* calls to MLIR math-dialect intrinsics
(SqrtOp, PowFOp, FloorOp, CeilOp, RoundOp, etc.) for optimal
performance. Integer operations (clamp, sign) use arith dialect ops
(MinSIOp, MaxSIOp, CmpIOp, SelectOp).

The Rust FFI crate (hew-std-math) provides fallback implementations
wrapping f64 methods from libm.

Includes E2E test covering all functions.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: hew
repository.description: A statically-typed, actor-oriented programming language for concurrent and distributed systems.

commit.message:
feat(stdlib): add std::net::dns for hostname resolution (#204)

Add DNS resolution module wrapping std::net::ToSocketAddrs:
- resolve(hostname) -> Vec<String> — all IP addresses
- lookup_host(hostname) -> String — first IP address

Rust FFI uses hew-cabi for C ABI string/vec interop. Includes
unit tests for localhost resolution, null/invalid input handling.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(stdlib): add std/sort module with sort and reverse for Vec (#208)

* feat(stdlib): add std/sort module with sort and reverse for Vec

Add type-specific sorting utilities:
- sort_ints: sort Vec<int> ascending
- sort_strings: sort Vec<String> alphabetically
- sort_floats: sort Vec<f64> ascending (NaN-aware)
- reverse: reverse Vec<int>

All functions return new vectors, leaving originals unchanged.
Includes Rust unit tests and E2E test (sort_basic).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Merge main and add hew-std-sort to the CI workflow's stdlib build step
so the static library is available for E2E test linking.

commit.message:
feat(stdlib): add std::net::tls for TLS client connections (#205)

* feat(stdlib): add std::net::tls for TLS client connections

Implement TLS client module wrapping rustls with ring crypto provider,
following the same pattern as std::net::quic. Provides:

- connect(host, port) -> TlsStream — open TLS connection with webpki roots
- write(stream, data) -> int — write bytes to stream
- read(stream, size) -> bytes — read bytes from stream
- close(stream) — close connection and free resources

Includes Rust unit tests for null-safety on all FFI entry points.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: merge main and apply formatting

Merge main to pick up labelled loop changes. Apply cargo fmt to fix
function signature formatting in std/net/tls/src/lib.rs.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: hew
repository.description: A statically-typed, actor-oriented programming language for concurrent and distributed systems.

commit.message:
fix(stdlib): add unsafe wrappers and fix type coercions for type-check compliance (#211)

Wrap all extern FFI calls in `unsafe { }` blocks across 31 stdlib
modules. Add explicit `as i32` casts where the type checker now rejects
implicit i64-to-i32 coercions (base64, csv, hex, jwt, path, semver,
string). Rewrite string.is_ascii to use char comparison instead of
unsupported char-to-int casts. Add stub return values in builtins.hew
so empty-bodied builtin declarations pass type-checking.

Update stdlib_loader to look through Expr::Unsafe nodes when extracting
FFI call targets from impl method bodies (required for handle-method
discovery after the unsafe wrapping).

All 42 std/*.hew files now pass `hew check`. All 459 codegen E2E tests
and 307 Rust unit tests continue to pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: hew
repository.description: A statically-typed, actor-oriented programming language for concurrent and distributed systems.

commit.message:
feat(stdlib): add std/io for stdin/stdout/stderr operations (#202)

* feat(stdlib): add std/io for stdin/stdout/stderr operations

Add std/io module with four functions:
- read_line() -> String: read a line from stdin
- write(s: String): write to stdout (no newline)
- write_err(s: String): write to stderr
- read_all() -> String: read all stdin

Runtime implementation in hew-runtime/src/stdio.rs uses Rust std::io.
Includes E2E test for write/write_err operations.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: merge main and apply formatting

Merge main to pick up labelled loop changes. Apply cargo fmt to fix
module ordering in hew-runtime/src/lib.rs.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(stdlib): add std::fmt module for number formatting utilities (#207)

Implement pure-Hew number formatting and string padding in std/fmt:

- to_hex(n) — integer to lowercase hexadecimal string
- to_binary(n) — integer to binary string
- to_octal(n) — integer to octal string
- pad_left(s, width, fill) — left-pad string to width
- pad_right(s, width, fill) — right-pad string to width
- repeat(s, count) — repeat string N times

All functions handle negative numbers (prefixed with "-") and zero.
Includes E2E test covering all functions with edge cases.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: hew
repository.description: A statically-typed, actor-oriented programming language for concurrent and distributed systems.

commit.message:
feat(stdlib): add std::collections::hashset module (#203)

Expose the existing HashSet runtime FFI as a stdlib module.

The hew-runtime already exports hew_hashset_* functions via #[no_mangle]
extern "C" symbols. This commit adds the Hew-side API that wraps those
FFI bindings with an opaque HashSet handle type, trait methods, and a
module-level constructor.

API: new, insert_int, insert_string, contains_int, contains_string,
remove_int, remove_string, len, is_empty, clear, free.

Includes E2E test covering all operations with both integer and string
element types.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: hew
repository.description: A statically-typed, actor-oriented programming language for concurrent and distributed systems.

commit.message:
feat(stdlib): add std::testing assertion library (#201)

Add a pure Hew testing assertion library with the following functions:

- assert_eq(actual, expected) — integer equality
- assert_eq_str(actual, expected) — string equality
- assert_true(condition) — panic if false
- assert_false(condition) — panic if true
- assert_ne(a, b) — panic if equal
- assert_gt(a, b) — panic if a <= b
- assert_lt(a, b) — panic if a >= b

All assertions panic with descriptive messages on failure. No Rust FFI
needed — this is pure Hew.

Includes an E2E test exercising every assertion function.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: hew
repository.description: A statically-typed, actor-oriented programming language for concurrent and distributed systems.

docs: update README with accurate structure, CI/CD docs, remove dead links

- Updated repo structure to include Tests/, Dev/, .github/, build script
- Removed ghost dependencies (Microsoft.Graph.Groups, powershell-yaml)
- Removed dead file references (Examples/, VERIFICATION_REPORT, LICENSE)
- Updated contributing section with Pester test instructions
- Updated version status description

feat: extend runtime contract for lease-aware local sessions

- Add heartbeat/missedHeartbeatLimit validation in config
- Add in-memory lease manager in runtime server
- Add POST /v1/runtime/heartbeat endpoint

- Extend session-close to remove session lease

- Wire heartbeat config flags to oasclird
